what_it_does: Records metadata for an image attachment in the design-assets channel, linking an attachment id to a target image URI and an added timestamp.
observations: Markdown file with YAML frontmatter and no body content
standouts: Target URI contains literal spaces and parentheses in the filename 'image (47)'
The file is a bare attachment stub with no descriptive body
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata
Follows an id prefix pattern att-*
Conforms to a channel-memory attachment record layout
Target URI is not URL-escaped for spaces/parentheses
concerns: Target URI contains unescaped spaces and parentheses that may break parsing or resolution
No title, alt text, or description for the image
Generic filename 'image (47)' gives no semantic hint about the asset
notes: A bare attachment metadata stub that points to an unnamed image asset in the design-assets channel.
questions: What does image (47) depict or represent?
Is the target URI resolvable by the system?
Why is there no title, alt text, or added_by field?
purpose_vs_actual: The file is meant to record an image attachment; its content is minimal but consistent with an attachment stub.
intention_signals: Register an image asset from the design-assets channel in the ledger so it can be referenced or retrieved later.
novelty: The target URI uses literal spaces and parentheses instead of a normalized identifier; otherwise it is a standard attachment stub.
contribution: A timestamped link between an attachment id and a specific image asset in the design-assets channel, preserving provenance of when it was added.
